levels.label: Reactive
levels.copy: The Founder Operating System pattern is still mostly informal or ad hoc. Pick one stabilising action, document it, and avoid adding a long process before the basics are real.

levels.label: Busy
levels.copy: Some Founder Operating System practices exist, but they still depend on individual memory and effort. The next step is two repeatable routines with clear owners.

levels.label: Structured
levels.copy: Core Founder Operating System practices are repeatable, but evidence quality and ownership are uneven. Work on the weakest dimension before optimising the strongest one.

levels.label: High-Leverage
levels.copy: Founder Operating System practices are measured and connected to decisions. The main work is now removing the constraint that prevents the system from scaling cleanly.

levels.label: Compounding
levels.copy: Founder Operating System is operating as a learning system. Keep calibrating against evidence, stress-test resilience, and avoid treating a strong score as a permanent guarantee.

recommendations.focus-and-trade-offs: Reduce the quarter to three ranked outcomes and publish a founder stop-doing list.
recommendations.decision-cadence: Define decision owners and review rules for the five most frequent decisions.
recommendations.metrics-and-evidence: Build a one-page driver tree linking company outcomes to leading and guardrail metrics.
recommendations.personal-sustainability: Audit two weeks of calendar data and remove, automate, or delegate the lowest-leverage 20%.

defaultSecondAction: Retake the diagnostic after a focused 30-day improvement cycle and compare only your own progress unless a team comparison is explicitly agreed.
reflectionQuestion: Which assumption behind your weakest dimension deserves the fastest evidence check?
methodologyNote: The current version uses four dimensions with two questions each. Dimension scores are normalised to 0-100. The balanced index weights 70% average dimension performance and 30% the weakest dimension. No percentile or benchmark is shown until a compatible sample exists.
shareNote: Share cards reveal only the level and strongest area. They do not publish individual answers, sensitive weaknesses, legal or financial details, security gaps, or family-specific information.
